description: Contract in which Ṣedaqa b. Muvḥar, a scholar known from a number of manuscripts dating to 1076–86 CE (e.g. T-S 20.110 (PGPID 1774)) gives 50 dinars to his elder daughter Sitt al-Aqrān Mufaddāt and 25 dinars to his younger daughter Sitt al-Yumn Taqiyya. In the scribal hand of Hillel b. ʿEli (fl. 1066–1108 CE). The money is destined for their trousseaus when they reach maturity, but will in the meantime be supervised by the oil merchant (zayyāt) Abū Manṣūr Aharon b. Mevasser in the form of a partnership to which Aharon himself contributes the small sum of 5 dinars. The total capital of 80 dinars is ensured as a "deposit of the court," for which the highest possible form of security had to be given. Dated to the last third of the 11th century. (Information from Goitein's attached notes and Mediterranean Society, I, pp. 175–76, 442)
description_he: חוזה שבו צדקה ב' מובחר, חכם הידוע ממספר כתבי יד (1076–1086), נותן 50 דינרים לבתו הבכורה סת אל-אקראן ו-25 לבתו הצעירה סת אל-יומן. הכסף ישמש לנדוניותיהן ובינתיים ינוהל על ידי אהרון ב' מבשר כשותפות. בכתב הלל ב' עלי.
